overview: A minimal single-page web application that serves a static 'Hello World' message to visitors. The architecture consists of a single frontend component built with TypeScript and React, deployed as a static web application that renders the message directly in the browser without requiring any backend services.
requirements: The application must display 'Hello World' text when a visitor accesses the page
The application must be accessible via a web browser
The application must load and render the message without errors
The application must provide a health check endpoint for monitoring

components.name: hello-world-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: A simple web application that displays Hello World
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a TypeScript React single-page application that displays 'Hello World'. Use Create React App or Vite as the build tool. The main App component should render a div or h1 element containing the text 'Hello World' centered on the page. Include basic CSS styling to center the text both horizontally and vertically on the viewport. The application should be served on port 3000 during development. Create a Dockerfile that uses a multi-stage build: first stage uses node:18-alpine to build the React app with npm install and npm run build, second stage uses nginx:alpine to serve the static files from /usr/share/nginx/html. Copy the build output to the nginx html directory. Create an nginx.conf that serves the app on port 8080 and includes a /health endpoint that returns 200 OK. The health endpoint should return JSON with status:ok. Ensure all static assets are properly served and the application works without JavaScript errors. Handle 404s by serving index.html for client-side routing. Set appropriate cache headers for static assets. The application should be fully self-contained with no external API dependencies.
